books.title: The Great Gatsby
books.author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
books.description: A story of the mysteriously wealthy Jay Gatsby and his love for the beautiful Daisy Buchanan, told through the eyes of narrator Nick Carraway.

books.title: To Kill a Mockingbird
books.author: Harper Lee
books.description: The unforgettable novel of a childhood in a sleepy Southern town and the crisis of conscience that rocked it. Set in the mid-1930s.

books.author: George Orwell
books.description: Among the seminal texts of the 20th century, Nineteen Eighty-Four is a rare work that grows more haunting as its futuristic purgatory becomes more real.

books.title: Pride and Prejudice
books.author: Jane Austen
books.description: Since its immediate success in 1813, Pride and Prejudice has remained one of the most popular novels in the English language.

books.title: The Hobbit
books.author: J.R.R. Tolkien
books.description: A homebody hobbit in Middle Earth gets talked into joining a quest with a group of dwarves and a wizard to take back their homeland and treasure from a dragon.

books.title: Dune
books.author: Frank Herbert
books.description: Set on the desert planet Arrakis, Dune is the story of the boy Paul Atreides—who would become known as Muad'Dib—and of a great family's ambition to bring to fruition humankind's most ancient and unattainable dream.

reviews.comment: A timeless classic with beautiful prose.
One of the greatest American novels ever written.
